This is not legal advice, external counsel review, or procurement-grade legal approval; candidate labels remain separate from locked recall baselines.

_label_note: AUTO-LABELED by LLM. Spot-check before refreshing recall.lock.json. Lock updates must include this provenance in --reason per item 16.
_label_tier_note: US Stage B cleanup: must_detect is the strict detector-aligned benchmark tier; ideal_must_detect preserves broader auto-labeled statutory coverage for gap discovery. ideal_must_detect is not a locked recall baseline. Stage B labels are project-owner approved for internal benchmarking.

_strict_reconciliation.basis: US Stage B auto-label strict tier was reconciled to deterministic strict-mode engine findings; original auto-label breadth is preserved in ideal_must_detect for gap discovery. This is internal benchmark cleanup, not legal advice or procurement-grade review.

must_detect.matched_text:
misrouted compliance bulletin and an SFTP permission misconfiguration resulted in a routine capacity report being sent to a role mailbox at PineSpan Research LLC (sell-side).
The bulletin referenced internal policy names (Biometric Sign-In Standard v3, GenomeSafe Analytics Pilot) and used benign “playbook” and “endzone” metaphors common to our training materials.
Content review: the file held stale, public performance metrics already posted in our 05/01 website update and reflected in a Form 8-K furnished at that time.
Reg FD assessment: the communication mirrored public disclosures and was sent to an open analyst list; no selective disclosure of material non-public information as defined under Rule 10b-5.
GLBA/HIPAA/CCPA review: customer headers appeared as Acct#: 0000-XYZ-1234 and Name: Xxxxxx, with no SSNs, DOBs, addresses, or card PANs present.
However, one appended chat line stated, “Patient Naya Elling is scheduled for dialysis on 06/12,” which is PHI linked to a named individual and should not have been included.
Containment: recall request issued within 12 minutes; PineSpan confirmed deletion; SFTP keys rotated and write-only roles enforced.
Cyber checks: no unauthorized access detected; Brookline Edge gateway logs show zero exfil anomalies; CopperShield endpoint scans clean.
Operations: banking and HSA services remained available; our Marathon City branch re-opened at 10:00 ET as planned.
Notifications: HIPAA low-volume incident risk assessment initiated; GLBA/CCPA thresholds not met for consumer notice; SEC reporting and IR teams logged the event under Reg FD controls.
Remediation: “Clean Desk & Clean Share” control refreshed; de-identified exports now default; analyst distribution lists restricted to public-only briefs.
must_detect.reason: promoted from strict runtime finding during Stage B baseline reconciliation
